tasks.title: Insert Entry classification (4-way) section with inline-vs-change-flow hard boundary

subtasks.title: Insert ## Entry classification (4-way) heading before ## Session mode — auto vs interactive (HARD GATE)
Declare the four entry classes in order with one-line definitions and execution behavior: Conversational, Small inline, Recommend change flow, Explicit change flow
Insert explicit boundary statement between class 2 (Small inline) and class 3 (Recommend change flow) so the classifier does not collapse the two classes
Add ## Inline vs change-flow hard boundary subsection inside Entry classification
List the six hard triggers with action and target: 4-file, multi-file write, heavy test/build, risky/uncertain scope, long-session (~20 tool calls), incident (fresh-audit halt)
State that the boundary constrains execution, not conversation (status reads, comparisons, clarifications stay conversational regardless of size)
Allow inline→change-flow handoff mid-execution and require the handoff to be surfaced to the user (no silent handoffs)

Cite Gentle-AI line ranges in the subsection body: gentle-ai/internal/assets/opencode/sdd-orchestrator.md:18-64, gentle-ai/internal/assets/antigravity/sdd-orchestrator.md:36-76, gentle-ai/README.md:51-64
Reject size-bucket classifier terminology (no Small/Medium/Large, no XS/S/M/L); kiro:70-82 and windsurf:233-245 are explicitly NOT adopted

tasks.title: Add Managed-change trigger phrases reference subsection inside Entry classification

subtasks.title: Insert ## Managed-change trigger phrases reference subsection inside ## Entry classification (4-way), after the hard boundary subsection
List the canonical English phrases verbatim: do this as a change, implement this as a change, use change flow, use the change pipeline, run this through change
List the canonical Spanish phrases verbatim (neutral professional Spanish): hazlo con change flow, implementalo como un change, usá change flow
Add explicit bare-flow exclusion statement paired with NOT (bare flow alone is NOT a trigger unless context clearly means managed change)
Add status-read exclusion so 'how's the auth change going?' type messages stay in entry class 1 and do NOT trip similarity check
Present the phrase list as a static reference table for the orchestrator thread — no regex engine, tokenizer, or CLI flag
Cite Gentle-AI source for the bilingual phrase-list pattern: gentle-ai/internal/assets/opencode/sdd-orchestrator.md:100-160 (use SDD / hazlo con SDD precedent)

tasks.title: Extend Session mode section with per-change-flow-entry preflight rules

subtasks.title: Add bullet: ask the interactive/auto question on every change-flow entry (entry class 3 or 4 only) — do NOT carry cached mode from a prior change in the same session
Add bullet: skip the question when the same message contains interactive or auto verbatim (exact substring match, not fuzzy intent)
Add bullet: do NOT ask the mode question for entry class 1 (Conversational) or entry class 2 (Small inline) — unless inline crosses the hard boundary mid-execution and routes to class 3
Add bullet: when the user answers, the next phase starts immediately in the answered mode (similarity check, Start/Resume classify loop, or pipeline delegation) — do NOT re-ask per phase
Add bullet: cache key is the change-flow run (change name), not the session. A user who answered auto for change A must be re-asked for change B in the same session
Add bullet: phase-scoped approval preserved — auto mode applies to phase transitions, but the human review gate and interactive checkpoint still pause for approval

Cite Gentle-AI line ranges in the section body: gentle-ai/internal/assets/opencode/sdd-orchestrator.md:100-160 (SDD Session Preflight + Entry Routing) and :178-200 (Execution Mode + interactive checkpoint)
Preserve the ## Session mode — auto vs interactive (HARD GATE) heading unchanged — downstream interactive checkpoint and auto gatekeeper anchor on it
Confirm zero new CLI flags, zero new status tokens, zero new ai-harness.change-status.* envelope fields

tasks.title: Rewire Modes — start vs resume section and embed Similarity check before change-new subsection

subtasks.title: Strip Conversational from the route contract list (moved to entry class 1) and strip the duplicate Grill line (grill gate lives in its own section)

Keep Start and Resume as the only two commands issued from entry class 4 — preserve every existing constraint: CLI errors on collision, CLI errors on missing, Engram discovery index after start, route on nextRecommended, lean conversational when in doubt, never infer from folder presence
Add ## Similarity check before change-new subsection inside the rewired ## Modes — start vs resume section, before the Start/Resume commands
State the search order: Engram first (mem_search project-scoped with intent keywords), then .ai-harness/changes/*, then .ai-harness/archive/*

Document the four outcomes: active folder → recommend change-continue (stay conversational, no auto-resume); archived → default stop (user may request {name}.next or fresh name); stale Engram → ignore and create new; no match → create new
Document the CLI-is-source-of-truth rule: use change-continue (which errors on missing) as the existence oracle, NOT raw ls on .ai-harness/changes/*
Document orchestrator-recommends-never-auto-routes: surface the recommendation, then wait for explicit user confirmation before invoking change-continue, {name}.next, or change-new
Document Engram is read-only: similarity check uses mem_search and (when needed) mem_context; it MUST NOT create new Engram topics (Engram writes remain owned by individual phases)
Gate the similarity check to entry classes 3 and 4 only — entry classes 1 and 2 (including status reads mentioning a change name) MUST NOT fire it

tasks.title: Strengthen tests/test_renderers.py with behavioral assertions across Claude, OpenCode, and Copilot renderers

subtasks.title: Add a parametrized test class across the same renderer set used by existing change_orchestrator tests (Claude, OpenCode, Copilot) so a missing marker in any renderer fails with renderer + marker named
Assert the four entry classes appear in canonical order (Conversational → Small inline → Recommend change flow → Explicit change flow) in document order
Assert an explicit boundary statement between class 2 (Small inline) and class 3 (Recommend change flow)
Assert all six hard trigger labels appear: 4-file, multi-file write, heavy test/build, risky/uncertain scope, long-session, incident
Assert canonical English trigger phrases present: do this as a change, implement this as a change, use change flow
Assert canonical Spanish trigger phrases present: hazlo con change flow, implementalo como un change, usá change flow
Assert bare-flow exclusion statement appears in the body, paired with NOT (or equivalent negative-language token)
Assert mode preflight tokens in the Session mode section: ask on every change-flow entry, skip if the user provided interactive or auto (or equivalent skip-when-explicit language)
Assert similarity-check tokens in the Similarity check subsection: Engram, .ai-harness/changes/, .ai-harness/archive/, active, archived, stale

Assert the five pinned Gentle-AI line references are present and reject any invented gentle-ai/ paths: README.md:51-64, opencode/sdd-orchestrator.md:18-64, :100-160, :178-200, antigravity/sdd-orchestrator.md:36-76
Assert the ## Session mode — auto vs interactive (HARD GATE) heading is preserved as a ##-level heading (downstream interactive checkpoint and auto gatekeeper anchor on it)
Assert no new CLI commands, flags, or status tokens — compare rendered body against a frozen pre-change snapshot of CLI markers; renames or additions fail the test
Preserve every pre-existing keyword-presence assertion currently in tests/test_renderers.py — new assertions are additive, not substitutive
Run uv run pytest tests/test_renderers.py -k change_orchestrator and confirm all renderer assertions pass
Run uv run pytest tests/test_change.py and confirm CLI surface tests pass without edits to tests/test_change.py
Run uv run pytest tests/test_renderers.py -k 'opencode or claude or copilot' render parity sweep — fails if any renderer silently drops a heading or mangles a marker
